channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man sentenced to life imprisonment with 25-year minimum for the murder of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte in Boston
item.description: Deividas Skebas, a 26-year-old Lithuanian man, has been sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 25 years for the murder of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte. The tragic incident occurred on J...

item.title: Senegal's Prime Minister Introduces Bill to Increase Prison Sentences for Same-Sex Relations to Up to 10 Years
item.description: Senegal's Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko has submitted a draft bill to parliament that would significantly strengthen existing laws criminalizing same-sex relations in the conservative, Muslim-majority ...

item.title: Norway's King Harald hospitalized in Tenerife due to infection and dehydration
item.description: King Harald V of Norway, the oldest living European monarch at 89, was hospitalized on Tuesday evening while on a holiday in Tenerife with his wife, Queen Sonja. The king fell ill due to an infection ...

item.title: Modi Visits Israel Amid Gaza War as India Balances Strategic Ties in the Middle East
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is undertaking a two-day visit to Israel, marking his first trip to the country since the war in Gaza began. During the visit, Modi is scheduled to address Israel's...

item.title: Thailand's Progressive Party Loses Ground to Established Political Families in Provincial Elections
item.description: In Thailand, the progressive People's Party (formerly Move Forward) suffered significant losses in the 2026 elections, particularly in provincial areas dominated by entrenched political families. Chut...

item.title: NHS Secures Alternative Bone Cement Supplies After Production Fault Disrupts Main Provider
item.description: The NHS has secured two alternative suppliers of medical bone cement after a production fault at its primary provider, Heraeus Medical, threatened to disrupt planned surgeries across the UK. The issue...

item.title: Ex-K-pop CEO Offers $18M Payout to Protect NewJeans from Legal Battles
item.description: Min Hee-jin, former CEO of the K-pop label Ador, has offered to give up a 25.6 billion won ($17.9 million) payout if Hybe, the parent company, drops all lawsuits against the girl group NewJeans. The c...

item.title: Tourette’s campaigner criticises BBC and Bafta after involuntary slur aired during awards ceremony
item.description: John Davidson, a Tourette’s campaigner from Galashiels, Scotland, has criticised the BBC for broadcasting an involuntary racial slur he shouted during the Bafta Film Awards ceremony. Davidson, whose c...

item.title: Queen Victoria statue in Belfast hospital vandalised with red paint by republican group
item.description: Police in Belfast have launched an investigation after a statue of Queen Victoria in the grounds of the Royal Victoria Hospital was targeted by vandals. The incident occurred on Friday afternoon when ...

item.title: Trump dissatisfied with nuclear talks as US and UK staff evacuate Iran amidst escalating tensions
item.description: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to rise, with President Donald Trump expressing dissatisfaction over nuclear talks with Tehran. Trump reiterated his stance that Iran must not be allowed to d...

item.title: NASA Postpones Artemis II Lunar Mission Launch to April Due to Rocket Issue
item.description: NASA has delayed the launch of Artemis II, the crewed lunar flyby mission, yet again, due to a technical issue with the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket. Originally scheduled for March 6, the new targ...

item.title: Paramount Skydance Agrees to Acquire Warner Bros Discovery in $110 Billion Deal After Netflix Withdraws Bid
item.description: Paramount Skydance has finalized a $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery, ending a competitive bidding war that involved Netflix. Netflix had initially agreed to acquire certain assets but...

item.title: Analysis of Trump's Record and Policies in His Lengthy State of the Union Address
item.description: In this Americast episode, the team discusses former President Donald Trump's unusually long State of the Union address, which lasted nearly two hours, making it the longest in history. The speech foc...

item.title: Mexico assures safety for 2026 World Cup fans despite recent cartel violence
item.description: Mexico's government has reassured international visitors that attending the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be safe, despite recent violent incidents linked to organized crime. The reassurance comes after th...

item.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger Criticizes Trump’s Policies in State of the Union Rebuttal
item.description: Abigail Spanberger, recently elected as Virginia’s first female governor, delivered the Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, sharply criticizing his economic and...

item.title: Man arrested for defacing Winston Churchill statue in Westminster with graffiti
item.description: A man has been charged after spray-painting the statue of Sir Winston Churchill in Parliament Square, Westminster, with the words 'Zionist war criminal' and other offensive graffiti. The incident occu...

item.title: Legendary Singer-Songwriter Neil Sedaka Dies at 86
item.description: American singer-songwriter Neil Sedaka, famous for timeless hits like 'Breaking Up Is Hard to Do' and 'Laughter in the Rain,' has passed away at the age of 86. His family released a heartfelt statemen...

item.title: The Changing Nature of Remembrance: From Honor to Loyalty Tests
item.description: In the article, the author argues that remembrance, particularly around events like the Holocaust, has shifted from being a solemn act of honoring victims to a performative loyalty test. The piece hig...

item.title: Workday Forecasts Slower Revenue Growth Despite CEO’s Optimism About Agentic AI
item.description: Workday CEO Aneel Bhusri used the company’s fourth-quarter earnings call—his first since returning to the role—to emphasize confidence in the company’s strategic direction, particularly its push into ...

item.title: Mental Health Benches Offering Support and Hope Across the UK
item.description: In 100 parks across the UK, benches illuminated by solar-powered lights and engraved with the message 'Someone is always listening' are offering hope to those struggling with mental health issues. The...
